metadata.notes: Proof verifier with chunked PDA buffer. A Honk proof is larger than the 1232-byte raw tx limit on Solana, so the program exposes an init/write/verify state machine. The buffer PDA is derived from seeds ["proof_buf", payer, salt(8)] under the verifier's own program id. Instruction data is NOT borsh: each ix is prefixed by a single u8 tag (0=Init, 1=Write, 2=Verify) followed by raw little-endian fields and the chunk bytes.

accounts.name: payer
accounts.desc: Funds the buffer PDA rent and signs the create_account CPI via PDA seeds.

accounts.name: buffer
accounts.desc: PDA at seeds=["proof_buf", payer, salt]; allocated to HEADER_LEN(8) + declared_len bytes.

args.name: chunk
args.desc: Raw bytes of the proof at [offset, offset+len). Subsequent writes may overlap or extend; the highest written tail is tracked in the buffer header for the Verify step.

accounts.name: buffer
accounts.desc: Read-only on this ix; must contain a fully written proof (written_len == declared_len).

fields.name: proof_bytes
fields.desc: Raw proof payload, length = declared_len once fully written.

errors.name: InvalidInstructionData
errors.msg: Empty data, unknown tag, declared_len out of range, or attempted write past declared_len.

errors.name: MissingRequiredSignature
errors.msg: Payer account is not a signer.

errors.name: InvalidSeeds
errors.msg: Buffer account does not match find_program_address(["proof_buf", payer, salt]).

errors.name: AccountDataTooSmall
errors.msg: Buffer length < HEADER_LEN; usually means Init was never confirmed before Write/Verify ran.

verdict_emissions.notes: On a successful Verify, two msg! lines are emitted in program logs:
lines: [ZK-JUDGE] STUB-VERIFIER accepted proof of <declared_len> bytes (entropy=<nonzero_count>). NOTE: cryptographic verification NOT performed by this stub.
